Title: A draft genome of the medicinal plant Cremastra appendiculata (D. Don) provides insights into the colchicine biosynthetic pathway

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaAER was identified as one of the 35 candidate colchicine-pathway genes in C. appendiculata. 2. The gene family showed a duplication event in the C. appendiculata lineage. 3. CaAER was included in the pseudobulb-associated purple co-expression module containing most colchicine pathway genes, supporting a role in active colchicine biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to colchicine biosynthesis by supporting formation of the phenylalanine-derived precursor 4-hydroxydihydrocinnamaldehyde, thereby promoting precursor supply for colchicine accumulation in pseudobulb.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal anti-gout alkaloid; also associated with anti-inflammatory and antitumor activities.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: pseudobulb, especially outer pseudobulb skin as cited in background
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Gene family duplicated; specific member-level expression not detailed
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Present in pseudobulb-associated purple co-expression module
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology-based pathway prediction; phylogenetic analysis; RNA-seq expression profiling; WGCNA co-expression analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue comparison across pseudobulb, stem, and leaf; plant age 2, 4, and 6 years
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for pathway reconstruction or stacking to enhance colchicine production once biochemical function is validated.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assigned as a colchicine-pathway candidate but not biochemically validated in this study; catalytic role inferred from pathway model and homology.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaNMT was identified among 35 candidate genes for colchicine biosynthesis based on homology to Gloriosa superba pathway genes. 2. CaNMT occurred in the pseudobulb-associated green-yellow co-expression module together with CaCCR and CaOMT4. 3. Its module association supports pseudobulb as an active colchicine biosynthetic site.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes colchicine biosynthesis as a methylation-related tailoring step in the pathway, contributing to colchicine accumulation in pseudobulb.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anti-gout, anti-inflammatory, and antitumor medicinal compound.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Contained in green-yellow module highly correlated with pseudobulb samples
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BLAST homology to validated GsNMTt; RNA-seq; WGCNA co-expression analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue comparison and developmental age comparison
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate for later biochemical validation and metabolic engineering of colchicine pathway.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is one of the experimentally tracked core candidate pathway-associated genes in Results, but exact catalytic/regulatory role was not validated here.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O-methyltransferase genes were highlighted as having an important role in colchicine biosynthesis. 2. Ten CaOMTs were identified and classified into three subfamilies; CaOMT1-3 members were represented in subfamily 3 and were expanded in the genome. 3. CaOMT1-3 genes showed pseudobulb-associated expression/co-expression, and CaOMT1-3 was among genes validated by qRT-PCR. 4. O-methyltransferase activity was specifically enriched among pseudobulb-upregulated genes, supporting OMT importance in pseudobulb colchicine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes colchicine biosynthesis; OMT activity is implicated as a key methylation determinant associated with pseudobulb colchicine production.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: late tailoring methylation steps in colchicine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers methyl group(s) to colchicine-pathway intermediates during alkaloid scaffold tailoring.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly modifies intermediates toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as an important pathway control component; exact rate-limiting status not fully established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Used clinically for gout; anti-inflammatory and antitumor activities
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ten CaOMTs identified; some members clustered on one scaffold; duplicated gene family
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: CaOMT members enriched in pseudobulb-associated purple and green-yellow modules; CaOMT1-3 validated by qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome-wide identification; phylogenetic analysis; differential expression enrichment; WGCNA co-expression; qRT-PCR validation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue comparison among pseudobulb, leaf, and stem; 2-, 4-, and 6-year plants
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Priority engineering target for enhancing colchicine content; suitable for gene stacking with upstream precursor-supply genes after biochemical verification of individual isoforms.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper collectively refers to CaOMT1, CaOMT2, and CaOMT3 as CaOMT1-3 because sequence alignment results were consistent. OMT was the only candidate enzyme class explicitly emphasized as playing an important role.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Three CaOMT4 genes were identified in OMT subfamily 1. 2. CaOMT4 was present in both pseudobulb-associated purple and green-yellow modules. 3. CAPP12145 belonged to a pseudobulb-positive module, strengthening the case that OMT genes act at active colchicine biosynthesis sites.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes colchicine accumulation by catalyzing methylation of pathway intermediates in pseudobulb.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly modifies intermediates toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important pathway component; exact rate-limiting role not established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: CAPP12145 and related CaOMT4 members associated with pseudobulb-positive modules
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate isoform set for functional screening to identify the best methyltransferase for colchicine pathway engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CAPP12144 and CAPP12145 are scaffold-linked with certain CaOMT1-3-related genes, consistent with local duplication/clustered organization.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Six CaPAL genes were identified, with four duplication events in the family. 2. CaPAL was expressed higher in pseudobulbs and stems than in leaves. 3. CaPAL was included in the pseudobulb-associated purple module and was validated by qRT-PCR. 4. The study discusses PAL as a speed-limiting step in phenylpropanoid metabolism, linking it to colchicine precursor formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports colchicine biosynthesis by increasing phenylalanine-derived precursor supply toward 4-hydroxydihydrocinnamaldehyde.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: phenylalanine metabolism feeding into colchicine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to cinnamic acid in the entry step of phenylpropanoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Discussed as a speed-limiting step in phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in pseudobulbs and stems than leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Pseudobulb/stem-biased; present in purple module; qRT-PCR supported expression pattern
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology-based identification; phylogeny; RNA-seq; co-expression; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong upstream candidate to combine with downstream tailoring genes such as OMTs for enhanced colchicine production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper links family expansion and duplication to pathway evolution.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ca4CL was one of the candidate enzymes in the phenylalanine-derived branch toward colchicine. 2. It was present in the pseudobulb-associated purple co-expression module. 3. Ca4CL was among genes selected for qRT-PCR validation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes colchicine biosynthesis by activating cinnamate-derived intermediates in the phenylalanine branch.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: phenylalanine metabolism feeding into colchicine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Present in pseudobulb-associated purple module; qRT-PCR tested
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology-based candidate identification; co-expression analysis; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for precursor-flux enhancement when combined with downstream colchicine tailoring genes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not biochemically validated in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaCCR was identified among candidate colchicine biosynthetic genes and the family showed one duplication event. 2. CaCCR occurred in both purple and green-yellow pseudobulb-associated modules. 3. Its repeated appearance across pseudobulb-positive modules supports a role in active colchicine precursor formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes colchicine biosynthesis by contributing to synthesis of the phenylalanine-derived aldehyde precursor branch.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: phenylalanine metabolism feeding into colchicine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces cinnamoyl-CoA-type intermediates to corresponding aldehydes for downstream precursor formation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Included in both pseudobulb-associated purple and green-yellow modules
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential precursor-supply engineering target in colchicine-producing tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific member identities were not detailed in the main text.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaTyDC/DDC genes were highly expressed in pseudobulbs. 2. CaTyDC/DDC was included in the pseudobulb-associated purple module. 3. CaTyDC/DDC was validated by qRT-PCR. 4. The paper places TyDC/DDC in the L-tyrosine-to-dopamine branch that feeds colchicine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes colchicine biosynthesis by supplying dopamine from L-tyrosine in the alkaloid precursor branch, with strong pseudobulb expression consistent with active biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: tyrosine metabolism feeding into colchicine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes decarboxylation in the tyrosine-derived branch leading to tyramine/dopamine formation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Pseudobulb-high; present in purple module; qRT-PCR supported
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling; co-expression analysis; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key target to boost tyrosine-derived precursor supply in engineered colchicine production systems.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper discusses analogous tissue-specific expression of TYDC in opium poppy for context.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaCYP76AD5 was identified as a candidate colchicine-pathway gene and the family showed one duplication event. 2. CaCYP76AD5 expression was high in leaves rather than pseudobulbs. 3. The gene participates in the L-tyrosine-to-dopamine branch of the proposed pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to dopamine-branch precursor formation for colchicine biosynthesis, although its higher leaf expression suggests tissue-specific pathway partitioning or non-biosynthetic roles.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: tyrosine metabolism feeding into colchicine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology-based identification; phylogeny; RNA-seq expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Needs functional and tissue-specific validation before deployment, because expression pattern does not match pseudobulb accumulation as clearly as OMT or TyDC/DDC.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Leaf-biased expression was a notable exception among candidate pathway genes.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A single CaDAHPS gene was identified in the genome. 2. CaDAHPS was expressed higher in pseudobulbs and stems than in leaves. 3. It is placed at the beginning of aromatic amino acid precursor supply feeding colchicine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports colchicine biosynthesis by supplying shikimate-pathway precursors for L-phenylalanine and L-tyrosine formation.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: shikimate pathway feeding into colchicine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in pseudobulbs and stems than leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology-based candidate identification; RNA-seq expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream precursor-supply target but likely requires careful balancing due to central metabolic role.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study specifically notes only a single DAHPS gene in C. appendiculata.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaCYP71FB1 was among the homologs identified using validated Gloriosa superba enzymes. 2. The CaCYP71FB1 gene family showed three duplication events, including two tandem duplications. 3. CaCYP71FB1 was present in the pseudobulb-associated purple module.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes colchicine biosynthesis through a late oxidative tailoring step, with family expansion suggesting evolutionary importance in C. appendiculata.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly modifies intermediates toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Family expanded with tandem duplication events
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BLAST homology to validated GsCYP71FB1; phylogeny; co-expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising late-pathway tailoring candidate for reconstructing colchicine biosynthesis after biochemical validation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The exact reaction was not directly tested in C. appendiculata.
